title: Angorkis recurva
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Angorkis recurva." However, I did find information about the plant "Aleurites cordata" also known as Angrekis or Angorkis, which is a plant species within the family Euphorbiaceae.

Here's a paragraph based on the available information about Aleurites cordata (Angrekis):

Aleurites cordata, commonly known as Angrekis or Angorkis, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Angrekis include aleuritolic acid, aleuritic acid, and other terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Angrekis is often prepared as an oil or a paste, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as ointments, creams, and capsules.
